meaning: vi.长肥,(靠...)发财,养肥vt.养肥,使肥沃,使充实,使肥胖
examples: She's very thin after her illness—but we'll soon fatten her up.
Birds will fatten themselves with heavy feeding in advance of a long migrational flight.
They would fatten themselves for another long leg of their journey.
Holding has integrated his ranching operation, purchasing two feedlots in Wyoming to fatten his own cattle.
High crude oil prices fatten the economic prospects of the oil services and transport stocks.
